text: Reworked the living-workspace example's journey.md (clarifying that a "hop" starts from the desired end state and making the start-to-end state delta visually explicit), then set PubMed up as the research backend in the seed material and added a PubMed search tool. Dogfooded by scaffolding a `working/` folder from the template and running an actual SLR, then spun that into a new public repo `openreview` in C:\code as an SLR-specific version of the workspace, alongside go-to-market work: multiple README rewrites (leading with what it is and who it's for, aimed at non-technical researchers) and a drafted Hacker News post framed around "living workspaces"/agent-native repos and Karpathy. Most of the session went into overhauling the SLR dashboard to mirror the exported report: a PRISMA flow with the five pipeline stages (information sources/search, deduplication, title-abstract screening, full-text, findings/eval) as tabs with a green/red bar on top, a reproducible search shown as plain query text by default instead of Python, and a records modal folded under the pipeline that also opens when a group is clicked. Finished by converting the export action into a real download button, fixing the contradictory "snapshot, not live" label, and adding navigation from a live report back to the index page.
